comment: Fixed scenario set for scripts/narrative_ab.js (v0.6.0 M4, plan D6). Each scenario exercises one craft dimension the prompt overhaul targets. Keep this set stable across prompt iterations so comparisons stay apples-to-apples.

scenarios.name: tavern_social
scenarios.tests: NPC voice + dialogue; not echoing the player

npcs.name: Brunhild
npcs.notes: dwarven innkeeper of the Gilded Flagon, gruff but fair

history.content: I step into the Gilded Flagon and shake the rain off my cloak.
Warmth and pipe smoke wrap around you. Behind the counter, Brunhild glances up from a tally book, one brow rising at the puddle forming under your boots.

scenarios.transcript: I ask Brunhild if anyone strange has passed through this week, and slide a silver across the counter.

scenarios.name: combat_tension
scenarios.tests: pacing under action; concrete, quick beats; yields the turn

history.content: I back toward the dead tree, arrow nocked.
Mud sucks at your heels. The marsh wolf circles left, hackles up, keeping low to the reeds — it has done this before.

scenarios.transcript: I loose the arrow at its shoulder and draw my knife in the same motion.

scenarios.name: exploration_atmosphere
scenarios.tests: sensory scene craft; discovery pacing; ends on a hook

quests.title: Find the source of the bells

history.content: I wade down the flooded stair into the chapel.

scenarios.transcript: I raise my torch and look around the nave.

scenarios.name: consequence_recall
scenarios.tests: world-fact safety: never contradict the snapshot or story so far

npcs.name: Elder Miro
npcs.notes: village elder; Kael returned the heron feather to him

npcs.name: Sera
npcs.notes: Miro's apprentice, killed by marsh wolves

scenarios.chapterSummary: Kael recovered the heron feather from Blackreed Marsh at great cost: Miro's apprentice Sera was killed by wolves on the journey. Miro received the feather with grief as much as gratitude.

history.content: I knock on Elder Miro's door at dusk.

scenarios.transcript: I ask Miro how he has been holding up since everything that happened.

scenarios.name: quiet_moment
scenarios.tests: low-stakes pacing; restraint; no manufactured drama

history.content: I make camp off the North Road as the light goes.
The fire takes on the third strike. Sparks drift up through the pines, and somewhere far off a night bird calls once, then thinks better of it.

scenarios.transcript: I sit by the fire and clean my gear, taking stock of where I'm headed and why.

scenarios.name: player_overreach
scenarios.tests: narrator agency: big outcomes need setup; complication without blocking

npcs.name: Captain Vesk
npcs.notes: garrison captain, armed, flanked by two guards

history.content: I walk into the garrison hall where Captain Vesk is holding court.

scenarios.transcript: I grab Vesk by the collar, tell everyone he runs the smuggling ring, and make him confess on the spot.
